title: Add Metadata to Promotion
body: Add Metadata to Promotion What this guide covers Attaches custom metadata (key value pairs) to a promotion for system integration or market specific tracking needs. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the promotion you want to update. Click the action menu button (three dots), then select Meta . Step 3: The Metadata Wizard will open. You can add both public and private metafields. Step 4: To add a metafield, click the + Add Metafield button. Enter the following: Field What to enter Notes Key The name of the metadata field e.g., “campaign id”, “region”. Defined by your technical team. Value The value for this field e.g., “CAMP123”, “APAC”. Must match the format your integrations expect. Public/Private Toggle to specify visibility Public metadata is visible to integrations. Private is for internal tracking. Step 5: Click the Save button to apply the metadata. :::note Only add metadata if your technical team has specified the exact keys and values required for your system integrations. Metadata is used to pass additional data to connected systems. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Attaches custom metadata (key value pairs) to a promotion for system integration or market specific tracking needs.

title: Advanced Promotions Search
body: Advanced Promotions Search What this guide covers Provides enhanced filtering and search capabilities across all promotions, allowing operators to locate specific promotions by complex criteria — useful when searching for all promotions that contain a specific item, tag, or condition. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Store Groups tab. Step 3: Click the Advanced Search button. Step 4: Select where to search for your target item by choosing from the dropdown: Option Meaning Requirements Search only within promotion requirements Effects Search only within promotion effects Both Search in both requirements and effects Step 5: Click the Search button to populate the results table. Step 6: Refine your search by selecting: Item Type — Filter by the type of item you’re searching for Promo Tag — Filter by promotion tag Then select the specific item or tag you want to search for. Step 7: Review the search results. The table will display: Promotion Name — The name of the matching promotion Description — The description entered when the promotion was created Match Count — How many times the item appears in requirements and/or effects :::tip If no results are found, try adjusting your search parameters or using different item types or tags. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Provides enhanced filtering and search capabilities across all promotions, allowing operators to locate specific promotions by complex criteria — useful when searching for all p...

title: Archive a Promotion
headings: To find and restore archived promotions
body: Archive a Promotion What this guide covers Marks a promotion as archived to remove it from active use while retaining its record for historical reporting. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the promotion you want to archive. Click the action menu button (three dots), then select Archive . Step 3: A warning dialog will appear stating that archiving the promotion will remove it from assigned store groups. Click the Yes, Archive Promotion button to confirm. :::caution Archiving will immediately deactivate the promotion across all assigned store groups and remove it from customer facing channels. The promotion record is retained for historical purposes. ::: To find and restore archived promotions Step 1: In the Promotions list, use the Archived filter column and select Yes to display all archived promotions. Step 2: Find the promotion you want to restore and click the promotion name to open the Promotion Wizard. Step 3: Click the Unarchive button to restore the promotion to active status. Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Marks a promotion as archived to remove it from active use while retaining its record for historical reporting.

title: Assign Promotions to Store Groups
body: Assign Promotions to Store Groups What this guide covers Links existing promotions to store groups, making them active for all stores within those groups simultaneously. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Assign Promotions to Store Groups button (or equivalent button on your screen). Step 3: Select the promotion(s) you want to assign. You can: Check the checkbox next to each promotion name Use the "Select All" option to select all visible promotions (or deselect using "Deselect All") Search for specific promotions using the search bar Step 4: Once you've selected your promotions, click Next or click on the next step indicator to proceed. Step 5: Select the store group(s) that should receive these promotions. You can: Check the checkbox next to each store group name Search for specific store groups using the search bar Step 6: Review your selections and click the Assign button to apply the promotions to the selected store groups. :::note Promotions can only be assigned to store groups, not to individual stores. Once assigned, the promotions become active immediately for all stores in those groups and are displayed on their digital ordering channels. ::: :::tip You can also assign promotions from the Store Groups section. See for that workflow. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Links existing promotions to store groups, making them active for all stores within those groups simultaneously.

title: Copy Promotion
body: Copy Promotion What this guide covers Duplicates an existing promotion as a starting point for a new one, reducing setup time for similar offers. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the promotion you want to copy. Click the action menu button (three dots), then select Copy . Step 3: The Promotion Wizard will open with all the details from the original promotion pre filled. Update the promotion details as needed: Promotion Name — The copied promotion defaults to “Copy of [Original Name]”. Change this to a unique name. Display Name — Update if needed. Description — Update if needed. Promotion Flow — Change if needed. Requirements — Add, remove, or modify as needed. Effects — Add, remove, or modify as needed. Step 4: Review all your changes and click the Create button to save the new promotion. :::note The copied promotion is independent of the original. Changes made to either promotion will not affect the other. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Duplicates an existing promotion as a starting point for a new one, reducing setup time for similar offers.

title: Copy Promotion ID
body: Copy Promotion ID What this guide covers Copies a promotion’s unique ID to your clipboard for use in support tickets, system integrations, or cross referencing with other systems. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the promotion you want to copy the ID for. Click the action menu button (three dots), then select Copy ID . Step 3: The promotion ID has been copied to your clipboard. You can now paste it into support tickets, integration forms, or other systems as needed. :::note The Promotion ID is a unique identifier assigned by Atlas. Technical teams use this ID to reference the promotion in integrations, debugging, and system records. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Copies a promotion’s unique ID to your clipboard for use in support tickets, system integrations, or cross referencing with other systems.

title: Create a Promotion
body: Create a Promotion What this guide covers Builds a new promotional rule in Atlas — defining the discount type, conditions, validity period, and applicable store groups — so it can be surfaced to customers through digital ordering channels. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the + Create New Promotion button. Step 3: Fill in the promotion details. Fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Promotion Name Internal name for this promotion e.g., "BOGO Zinger May 2024". Visible to operators only. Display Name Customer facing name shown on ordering channels e.g., "Buy 1 Get 1 Free Zinger". Keep it short and compelling. Description Explains the promotion to customers Shown on the ordering interface. Step 4: Select a Promotion Flow based on the type of promotion you are creating. Flow Use when... New Price You want to set a new fixed price for a qualifying item Buy 1 Get 1 Customer buys one item and receives another free or discounted Reduced Price You want to apply a percentage or fixed reduction Custom Promotion The promotion doesn’t fit any of the above flows Step 5: Add Requirements — the conditions a customer must meet to trigger the promotion. Based on your selected flow, recommended requirements will appear below the requirement selector. Click Add to include a recommended requirement, or select a requirement type from the dropdown and click Add Requirement to build a custom condition. Step 6: Add an Effect and fill in the effect details. The effect defines what discount or reward the customer receives when the requirements are met. Step 7: Review all entered information and click Create to save the promotion. :::note Promotions can only be assigned to a Store Group , not to an individual store. See after creating your promotion. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Builds a new promotional rule in Atlas — defining the discount type, conditions, validity period, and applicable store groups — so it can be surfaced to customers through digita...

title: Create a Store Group
body: Create a Store Group What this guide covers Creates a named grouping of stores that share menu, promotion, or tax configurations — a foundational structure for managing multiple locations at scale. (This guide creates a store group from the Promotions section. You can also create one from the Store Groups section in the main navigation.) Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Store Groups tab. Step 3: Click the + Create New Store Group button. Step 4: Enter the store group details. Fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Store Group Name A descriptive name for this group e.g., “NSW Franchise Group”, “Corporate Owned Stores”, “Breakfast Pilot 2024”. Should clearly differentiate from other groups. Store Group Tags Optional labels for filtering and reporting e.g., “pilot”, “corporate”, “franchise”. You can enter multiple tags. Step 5: Select stores to add to this group. Use the table provided to find and select stores: You can filter by Store Number, Store Name, or Franchise Code to quickly find specific stores. Toggle the switch next to each store name to add it to the group. A store can belong to multiple store groups. Step 6: Review the summary showing all selected stores and group details. Click Create to save the store group. :::note A store can belong to multiple store groups. Promotions, tax rules, and menus are all managed at the store group level and apply to all member stores. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Creates a named grouping of stores that share menu, promotion, or tax configurations — a foundational structure for managing multiple locations at scale.

title: Create Serialized Code
body: Create Serialized Code What this guide covers Generates unique single use promotional codes tied to a promotion, used for tracked redemption campaigns where each customer receives a distinct code. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the promotion for which you want to create serialized codes. Click the action menu button (three dots), then select Serialized Code . Step 3: Click the + Create New Serialized Code button (or the equivalent button shown on your screen). Step 4: Fill out the serialized code form with the following details: Field What to enter Notes Prefix Optional text to add to the start of each code e.g., “KFC ”, “BOGO2024 ”. Leave blank if no prefix is needed. Quantity Number of codes to generate e.g., “100”, “5000”. Determines how many unique codes are created. Expiry Date When the codes expire Select the date after which codes cannot be redeemed. Step 5: Click Generate Codes to create and add the codes to the serialized code list. :::note Serialized codes are unique, single use redemption codes. Each code can only be redeemed once. Once generated, codes are added to the promotion and ready for distribution to customers. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Generates unique single use promotional codes tied to a promotion, used for tracked redemption campaigns where each customer receives a distinct code.

title: Delete a Store Group
body: Delete a Store Group What this guide covers Permanently removes a store group from the system. Steps Step 1: Start by going to the Promotions screen by clicking here. Step 2: Click the Store Groups tab Step 3: Click the Action button then click on “Delete” Step 4: Click to delete Notes :::note You can search for the store group you want to delete ::: :::note You can cancel anytime if needed ::: :::note All tax rules and promotions tied to stores in this store group will be removed if deleted ::: Additional information Promotions Delete a Store Group This is the Promotions screen where you will see a list of all the promotions you have created, create new promotions, search for any you have created, edit and copy, add extra info in the Meta link and assign them to Store Groups. Promotions can only assigned to a Store Group and not a singular store. Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Permanently removes a store group from the system.

title: Edit a Promotion
body: Edit a Promotion What this guide covers Updates an existing promotion’s configuration, such as validity dates, discount values, or requirement and effect settings. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the promotion you want to edit by browsing the table or using the search bar. Click the promotion name to open the Promotion Wizard. Step 3: Update any promotion details (name, display name, description, flow type, requirements, effects, or validity period). Step 4: When you have finished making changes, click the Save button to apply your updates. :::note The Promotion Wizard guides you through each section. You can click on any step number to jump to that section or proceed sequentially. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Updates an existing promotion’s configuration, such as validity dates, discount values, or requirement and effect settings.

title: Edit a Store Group
body: Edit a Store Group What this guide covers Updates a store group's details or membership. Steps Step 1: Start by going to the Promotions screen by clicking here. Step 2: Click the Store Groups tab Step 3: Find the store group you want to edit and click the store group name link Step 4: Click save to save your changes. Notes :::note There are multiple ways to edit a store group. This is the way to do it through Promotions. You can also do it through Store Groups in the main navigation. ::: :::note Here you can see an overview of the name and description and associated stores ::: :::note Here you can update the name and description or store group tags ::: :::note Here you can add or remove stores ::: Additional information Promotions Edit a Store Group This is the Promotions screen where you will see a list of all the promotions you have created, create new promotions, search for any you have created, edit and copy, add extra info in the Meta link and assign them to Store Groups. Promotions can only assigned to a Store Group and not a singular store. Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Updates a store group's details or membership.

title: Find Serialized Code
body: Find Serialized Code What this guide covers Locates a specific serialized promotional code within Atlas, used when verifying redemption status, troubleshooting issues, or managing code validity. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Find Serialized Code button (usually visible near the promotions list). Step 3: Enter the serialized code you want to look up in the search field and click the Search button. Step 4: The system will display the code details including: Code Value — The actual code text Promotion Name — The promotion this code is tied to Status — Whether the code is active, redeemed, or voided Expiry Date — When the code expires Step 5 (Optional): If you need to deactivate a code, click the Void Code button. A voided code cannot be redeemed. :::note Use this feature to verify that a code exists, check its redemption status, or troubleshoot customer issues with specific codes. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Locates a specific serialized promotional code within Atlas, used when verifying redemption status, troubleshooting issues, or managing code validity.

title: Import Promotions for a Store Group
body: Import Promotions for a Store Group What this guide covers Bulk imports promotion assignments from one store group to another, used when setting up large promotion campaigns across many stores or duplicating promotions from an existing configuration. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Store Groups tab. Step 3: Find the store group that will receive the promotions (the destination group). This is the group you want to import promotions into. Step 4: Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name, then select Import Promotions . Step 5: A dialog will appear asking you to select the store group to import promotions from (the source group). Search for and select the store group that has the promotions you want to copy. Step 6: Review the import summary and click Save to complete the import. :::caution Important: Importing promotions will replace any existing promotions linked to the destination store group. The operation cannot be undone. Make sure you are importing to the correct store group before confirming. ::: :::tip If you want to import specific promotions rather than all of them, toggle off the “Import All” option and select individual promotions instead. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Bulk imports promotion assignments from one store group to another, used when setting up large promotion campaigns across many stores or duplicating promotions from an existing...

title: Promotions
body: Promotions This section covers all promotions related tasks in the Byte Commerce Admin Portal. Topics Guides — Practical field by field reference and common promotion recipes Promotions Store Groups Advanced Promotions Search
summary: Promotions

title: View Promotions for a Store Group
body: View Promotions for a Store Group What this guide covers Lists all promotions assigned to a store group for visibility and auditing purposes. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Promotions section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Store Groups tab. Step 3: Find the store group you want to view promotions for by browsing the table or using the search bar. Step 4: Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name, then select Promotions . Step 5: A drawer panel will open showing all promotions assigned to this store group. The list includes: Promotion Name — The customer facing name of the promotion Description — Brief description of the promotion Status — Whether the promotion is active or archived Step 6 (Optional): To remove a promotion from the store group, click the Unlink button next to the promotion name. You will be asked to confirm the removal. :::note Unlinking a promotion from a store group makes it immediately unavailable at all stores in that group. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Promotions
summary: Lists all promotions assigned to a store group for visibility and auditing purposes.

title: Assign Promotions
body: Assign Promotions What this guide covers Assigns one or more promotions to a store group, activating them across all member stores simultaneously. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Assign Promotions button (usually prominently displayed near the top of the page). Step 3: Select the promotion(s) you want to assign. You can: Check the checkbox next to each promotion name to select it Use "Select All" to select all visible promotions (or "Deselect All" to clear selections) Search for specific promotions using the search bar Step 4: Once you've selected your promotions, click the Next button or click on the next step indicator to proceed. Step 5: Select the store group(s) that should receive these promotions. You can: Check the checkbox next to each store group name Search for specific store groups by name or tag Step 6: Review your selections and click the Assign button to apply the promotions to the selected store groups. :::note Once assigned, the promotions become active immediately for all stores in the selected store groups and are displayed on their digital ordering channels. ::: :::tip You can also assign promotions from the Promotions section. See for that workflow. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Assigns one or more promotions to a store group, activating them across all member stores simultaneously.

title: Copy a Store Group
body: Copy a Store Group What this guide covers Duplicates a store group configuration as a starting point for a new group, copying stores and tags but creating an independent new store group. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the store group you want to copy by browsing the table or using the search bar. Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name. Step 3: Click Copy . Step 4: Update the store group details: Field What to enter Notes Store Group Name A new, unique name for this group The system copies the original name; you must change it. e.g., "NSW Franchise Group Copy". Store Group Tags Labels for filtering and reporting Tags from the original group are automatically included. You can add or remove as needed. Step 5: Review and adjust store membership if needed: Stores from the original group are automatically selected (toggle switches are ON) Toggle OFF to remove any stores you don’t want in the new group Toggle ON to add additional stores Use the "Show me included" filter to quickly view only selected stores Filter by Store Number, Store Name, or Franchise Code to find specific stores Step 6: Review the summary of all changes and click the Create button to save the new store group. :::note The copied store group is independent from the original. Changes made to either group will not affect the other. Store membership is automatically copied from the original group but can be modified before saving. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Duplicates a store group configuration as a starting point for a new group, copying stores and tags but creating an independent new store group.

title: Create a Store Group
body: Create a Store Group What this guide covers Creates a named grouping of stores that share menu, promotion, or tax configurations — a foundational structure for managing multiple locations at scale. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the + Create New Store Group button. Step 3: Enter the store group details. Fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Store Group Name A descriptive name for this group e.g., "NSW Franchise Group", "Corporate Owned Stores", "Breakfast Pilot 2024". Should clearly differentiate from other groups. Store Group Tags Optional labels for filtering and reporting e.g., "pilot", "corporate", "franchise". Useful for organizing and searching multiple store groups. Step 4: Select stores to add to this group using the stores table. You can: Filter by Store Number, Store Name, or Franchise Code to quickly find specific stores Toggle the switch next to each store name to add or remove stores from the group View which stores are already selected by looking for the checked switches Step 5: Review the summary of stores you’ve selected and other details. Click Create to save the store group. :::note A store can belong to multiple store groups simultaneously. Promotions, tax rules, and menus are all managed at the store group level and apply to all member stores. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Creates a named grouping of stores that share menu, promotion, or tax configurations — a foundational structure for managing multiple locations at scale.

title: Create Tax Rule Group
body: Create Tax Rule Group What this guide covers Creates a named tax rule group that bundles multiple related tax rules together, allowing you to organize and reuse tax configurations across store groups. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the store group where you want to create a tax rule group. Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name. Step 3: Click Taxes from the dropdown menu. Step 4: Click the Tax Rule Groups tab. Step 5: Click the + Create New Tax Rule Group button. Step 6: Fill in the tax rule group details. Fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Tax Rule Group Name Descriptive name for this group e.g., "Standard GST Group", "NSW Reduced Tax Rules". Should indicate what taxes are included. Display Name Name shown in the interface Usually same or similar to the Tax Rule Group Name. Description Optional explanation of the group's purpose e.g., "GST rules applicable to New South Wales locations". Step 7: Click the Create Tax Group button to save the tax rule group. :::note Once created, a tax rule group is independent and can be used across multiple store groups. You can edit, copy, and delete tax rule groups at any time from this screen. ::: :::tip After creating a tax rule group, you'll need to add individual tax rules to it. See for that step. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Creates a named tax rule group that bundles multiple related tax rules together, allowing you to organize and reuse tax configurations across store groups.

title: Create Tax Rules
body: Create Tax Rules What this guide covers Defines individual tax rules within a store group, specifying the tax rates, conditions, and product filters applied to items sold at member stores. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the store group where you want to create a tax rule. Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name. Step 3: Click Taxes from the dropdown menu. Step 4: Click the + Create New Tax Rule button. Step 5: Fill in the basic tax rule information. Fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Tax Name Internal name for this rule e.g., "Standard GST 10%", "Delivery Surcharge Tax". Visible to operators. Effective From Date Date this rule takes effect You can use past dates for historic records. Format: DD/MM/YYYY. Tax Rule Group Assign to an existing or new group Optional. Groups multiple related tax rules together for easier management. Step 6: Add conditions that trigger this tax rule. This section is optional. Field What to enter Notes Cart Conditions Select conditions based on overall order e.g., "Order contains delivery", "Cart total exceeds $50". Choose from dropdown. Product Filters Select which products/categories this applies to e.g., "Only menu items in Burgers category". Choose from dropdown. Step 7: Click the + Add Tax button to define the tax calculation. Step 8: Fill in the tax calculation details: Field What to enter Notes Rate Mode Choose how tax is calculated Percentage (e.g., 10% GST) or Fixed Amount (e.g., $0.50 per item) Applied To What the tax applies to e.g., Subtotal, Delivery Fee, or specific item categories Tax Amount % Enter the tax rate as a percentage For percentage mode only. Enter numbers only (e.g., for 10% GST) Step 9: (Optional) Fill out Taxes on External Fees if you need to apply taxes to fees from third party delivery platforms. Step 10: Click to create the tax rule. A review screen will show all information you've entered. Click Create to save. :::note You can click on any step number in the wizard to navigate to that section without losing your changes. You can also edit, copy, and delete tax rules after creation. ::: :::tip Create a Tax Rule Group first if you want to organize related tax rules together. See for instructions. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Defines individual tax rules within a store group, specifying the tax rates, conditions, and product filters applied to items sold at member stores.

title: Delete a Store Group
body: Delete a Store Group What this guide covers Permanently removes a store group from the system, along with all associated promotions and tax rules. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the store group you want to delete by browsing the table or using the search bar. Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name. Step 3: Click Delete . Step 4: A confirmation dialog will appear. Review the warning message and click the Delete Store Group button to confirm permanent deletion. :::caution This action is permanent and cannot be undone. Deleting a store group will: Remove the store group from the system Unlink all promotions assigned to this store group Unlink all tax rules associated with this store group The member stores themselves are not deleted, only their grouping and assignments :::tip If you need to keep the store group configuration for future use, consider copying it before deletion. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Permanently removes a store group from the system, along with all associated promotions and tax rules.

title: Edit a Store Group
body: Edit a Store Group What this guide covers Updates a store group’s details, name, store membership, or associated tax rules. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the store group you want to edit by browsing the table or using the search bar. Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name. Step 3: Click Edit . Step 4: You will see the Store Group Editor showing all current details and store membership. Step 5: Update the store group details as needed: Field What to update Notes Store Group Name Descriptive name for the group e.g., "NSW Franchise Group". Store Group Tags Labels for filtering and reporting e.g., "pilot", "corporate". Step 6: Update store membership by toggling switches next to store names: Toggle ON to add a store to the group Toggle OFF to remove a store from the group Filter by Store Number, Store Name, or Franchise Code to find specific stores quickly Step 7: Review the summary showing all changes made during this session. Click Save to apply the updates. :::note You can click on any step number in the wizard to jump to that section without losing your changes. ::: :::tip If this store group has tax rules, you can view and edit them by clicking the action menu button and selecting Taxes . ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Updates a store group’s details, name, store membership, or associated tax rules.

title: Edit Promotions
body: Edit Promotions What this guide covers Adds or removes promotion assignments for a specific store group, allowing you to modify which promotions are active at member stores. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the store group whose promotions you want to modify. Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name. Step 3: Click Promotions from the dropdown menu. Step 4: A promotions drawer will open. Click the Edit Promotions button to enter edit mode. Step 5: Make changes to the promotion assignments: To add a promotion: Check the checkbox next to promotion names you want to add To remove a promotion: Uncheck the checkbox next to promotion names you want to remove Search for specific promotions using the search bar Toggle the "Show other promotions" switch to view promotions not currently assigned (if checked, you can see all available promotions; if unchecked, you see only currently assigned ones) Step 6: Review your changes in the summary section showing promotions added and removed. Click the Assign button to apply the changes. (The Assign button is only enabled after you make changes.) :::note Changes to promotion assignments take effect immediately for all stores in this store group. ::: :::tip You can search promotion names in the search bar to quickly find promotions you want to add or remove. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Adds or removes promotion assignments for a specific store group, allowing you to modify which promotions are active at member stores.

title: Import Promotions for a Store Group
body: Import Promotions for a Store Group What this guide covers Bulk imports promotion assignments from one store group to another for large scale campaign setup or configuration duplication. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the store group that will receive the promotions (the destination group). Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name. Step 3: Click Import Promotions from the dropdown menu. Step 4: A dialog will open. Select the store group you want to import promotions from (the source group). You can search for it using the search bar. Step 5 (Optional): Decide whether to import all or specific promotions: Option Action Import All Leave the toggle ON to import every promotion from the source group Import Specific Toggle OFF the "Import All" switch, then check the boxes next to only the promotions you want to import Step 6: Review the import summary and click Save to complete the import. :::caution Important: Importing promotions will replace any existing promotions linked to the destination store group. This action cannot be undone. Make sure you are importing to the correct store group before confirming. ::: :::tip If you only want to add specific promotions without replacing existing ones, use the workflow instead. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Bulk imports promotion assignments from one store group to another for large scale campaign setup or configuration duplication.

title: Store Groups
body: Store Groups This section covers all store groups related tasks in the Byte Commerce Admin Portal. Topics Store Groups Taxes Store Groups Promotions
summary: Store Groups

title: Unassign Promotions from Store Group
body: Unassign Promotions from Store Group What this guide covers Removes one or more promotions from a store group, immediately deactivating them across all member stores. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Find the store group whose promotions you want to remove. Click the action menu button (three dots) next to the store group name, then click Promotions . Step 3: A drawer will open showing all promotions currently assigned to this store group. To remove a promotion, click the Unassign button next to the promotion name. Step 4: A confirmation dialog will appear. Click Unassign Promotion to confirm removal of the promotion from this store group. :::caution Unassigning a promotion immediately deactivates it across all stores in this store group. Customers will no longer see this promotion on their digital ordering channels. ::: :::tip If you want to add or remove multiple promotions at once, use the workflow instead. ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Removes one or more promotions from a store group, immediately deactivating them across all member stores.

title: View Stores in a Store Group
body: View Stores in a Store Group What this guide covers Lists all stores currently belonging to a store group for visibility and verification purposes. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Store Groups section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Browse the Store Groups table to find the group you want to view, or use the search bar to locate it. Click on the store group name to view its details. Step 3: A detailed view will open showing: Store Group Name — The name of the selected group Store Group Tags — Any tags assigned to this group Stores Table — A complete list of all stores in this group The table displays: Store Number — Unique identifier for each store Store Name — Name of the store location Franchise Code — Franchise identifier if applicable Tax Rules — Whether this group has associated tax rules :::note The stores table lists all stores currently belonging to this store group. To modify store membership, see . ::: Related guides Part of the · Section: Store Groups
summary: Lists all stores currently belonging to a store group for visibility and verification purposes.

title: Accept Online Orders (Turn On or Off)
body: Accept Online Orders (Turn On or Off) What this guide covers Toggles whether a store accepts online orders through digital channels — used to quickly disable ordering during closures or operational issues. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Search for the store by Name , Store Number , or Franchise Code using the search box. Step 3: Once you find the store, click the store name (or any blue hyperlink) to view the store details, or click the three dot menu (•••) icon and select Edit . Step 4: Locate the Accepting Online Orders toggle and set it to your desired state: Yes : Store accepts online orders through digital channels No : Store does not accept online orders (orders are temporarily disabled) Step 5: Click the Save button to apply the change. :::tip Use No to quickly disable ordering during store closures, system maintenance, or staff shortages without needing to edit other store settings. ::: :::caution Clicking Cancel at any time discards your change. ::: Related guides — Update other store information Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Toggles whether a store accepts online orders through digital channels — used to quickly disable ordering during closures or operational issues.

title: Appear in Search Result (Turn On or Off)
body: Appear in Search Result (Turn On or Off) What this guide covers Controls whether a store appears in customer facing search results, allowing operators to hide a location without fully removing it. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Search for the store by Name , Store Number , or Franchise Code using the search box. Step 3: Once you find the store, click the store name (or any blue hyperlink) to view the store details, or click the three dot menu (•••) icon and select Edit . Step 4: Locate the Appear in Store Search Results toggle and set it to your desired state: Yes : Store appears in customer search results No : Store is hidden from customer search results but is not deleted Step 5: Click the Save button to apply the change. :::tip Use No to temporarily hide a location from customers while keeping it in Atlas for administrative purposes (e.g., during renovations, seasonal closures, or when preparing a new location before launch). ::: :::caution Clicking Cancel at any time discards your change. ::: Related guides — Update other store information — Control order acceptance separately Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Controls whether a store appears in customer facing search results, allowing operators to hide a location without fully removing it.

title: Allow Future Orders (Turn On or Off)
body: Allow Future Orders (Turn On or Off) What this guide covers Enables or disables a store’s ability to accept orders placed in advance, relevant for pre order or scheduled delivery capabilities. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Search for the store by Name , Store Number , or Franchise Code using the search box. Step 3: Once you find the store, click the store name (or any blue hyperlink) to view the store details, or click the three dot menu (•••) icon and select Edit . Step 4: Locate the Allows Future Orders toggle and set it to your desired state: Yes : Customers can place orders in advance for scheduled delivery or pickup No : Customers can only place orders for immediate fulfillment Step 5: Click the Save button to apply the change. :::note Future Orders Requirement: This setting requires the store to have a supported ordering channel configured. Confirm with your regional manager if your store supports this capability. ::: :::caution Clicking Cancel at any time discards your change. ::: Related guides — Update other store information Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Enables or disables a store’s ability to accept orders placed in advance, relevant for pre order or scheduled delivery capabilities.

title: Add Metafields to a Menu
body: Add Metafields to a Menu What this guide covers Attaches public and private custom metadata (key value pairs) to a store's menu for integration with downstream systems or market specific requirements. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Search for the store by Name , Store Number , or Franchise Code using the search box. Step 3: Once you find the store, click the three dot menu (•••) icon to open the options menu. Step 4: Click Meta from the dropdown menu. This opens the metafields management screen. Step 5 Public Metafields: Click the Add Public Metafield button (or tile) to add a public metafield. Step 6: Fill in the metafield details: Field What to enter Notes Key The field name or identifier e.g., "api version", "integration id" Value The field value e.g., "v2", "12345" Step 7: Click Add Store Metafields to save the public metafield. Step 8 Private Metafields: Click the Add Private Metafield button (or tile) to add a private metafield. Step 9: Fill in the metafield details using the same field structure as above. Step 10: Click Add Store Metafields to save the private metafield. :::note When to use metafields: Only add metafields if your technical team has specified key value pairs for system integration. Public metafields are visible to external systems; private metafields are for internal use only. ::: :::tip You do not need to add both public and private metafields. Add only the metafields required for your store's integrations. ::: Related guides — See menu assignments Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Attaches public and private custom metadata (key value pairs) to a store's menu for integration with downstream systems or market specific requirements.

title: Assign New Menu
body: Assign New Menu What this guide covers Links a published menu to a store's ordering channel (e.g., Digital, Kiosk, In Store), determining what customers see when ordering from that location. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Search for the store by Name , Store Number , or Franchise Code using the search box. Step 3: Once you find the store, click the three dot menu (•••) icon to open the options menu. Step 4: Click Menus from the dropdown menu. Step 5: Click the more menu button (⋯) or + Assign New Menu button to reveal additional options. Step 6: Click Assign New Menu . Step 7: Select a published menu from the list. This menu will be assigned to a specific ordering channel for this store. Field What to enter Notes Menu Select from published menus Only published menus appear here Channel Select the ordering channel e.g., Digital, Kiosk, In Store Step 8: Click Assign to confirm the assignment. :::tip Only published menus are available for assignment. If the menu you need is not in the list, publish it first using . ::: :::note Each store can have different menus assigned to different channels. For example, your Digital channel might use one menu while your In Store Kiosk uses another. ::: Related guides — See which menus are assigned to a store — Make a menu live after assignment Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Links a published menu to a store's ordering channel (e.g., Digital, Kiosk, In Store), determining what customers see when ordering from that location.

title: Create a Beneficiary
body: Create a Beneficiary What this guide covers Sets up a charitable beneficiary linked to specific stores, enabling donation collection or charity campaign functionality at those locations. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Beneficiaries tab at the top of the Stores page. Step 3: Click the + Create New Beneficiary button. Step 4: Fill in the beneficiary form using the field descriptions below. Fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Beneficiary Name Name of the charity or cause e.g., “KFC Youth Foundation” Accepting Donations Toggle: Yes or No Set to Yes to activate donation collection at selected stores Stores Search and select stores to associate with this beneficiary Use the search box to find stores by name, number, or code Step 5: To activate donation collection for this beneficiary, toggle Accepting Donations to Yes . Step 6: Use the Stores field to search for and select the stores where this beneficiary should be available. You can use the filter options to narrow the store list by store group or other criteria. Step 7: Once all required fields are completed, the Create Beneficiary button becomes active. Click Create Beneficiary to save the new beneficiary. :::tip Before creating a new beneficiary, use the search box to confirm a beneficiary with the same name doesn't already exist. A green checkmark in the “Accepting Donations” column indicates a beneficiary is active. ::: :::caution Clicking Cancel at any time discards all unsaved information. ::: Related guides — See all beneficiaries linked to a store — Update or remove a beneficiary Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Sets up a charitable beneficiary linked to specific stores, enabling donation collection or charity campaign functionality at those locations.

title: Create a Store
body: Create a Store What this guide covers Registers a new store location in Atlas with its required details, making it available for menu assignment and digital ordering. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the + Create New Store button in the top right corner of the screen. Step 3: Fill in the store details form. Fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Store Name Full display name of the store e.g., “KFC George Street Sydney” Store Number Unique numeric identifier assigned by market operations Must match the POS assigned store number Franchise Code Alphanumeric code identifying the franchisee Provided by your regional manager Time Zone The store’s local time zone Required for item snooze and future order accuracy Step 4: Once all required fields are completed, the Save button becomes active. Click Save to create the store. :::tip Before creating a new store, search the Stores list by Name, Number, or Franchise Code to confirm it doesn’t already exist. ::: :::caution Clicking Cancel at any time discards all unsaved information. ::: Related guides — Update the store after creation — Link a menu to the new store Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Registers a new store location in Atlas with its required details, making it available for menu assignment and digital ordering.

title: Edit Patch List
body: Edit Patch List What this guide covers Manages the ordered list of patches applied to a store's menu, allowing operators to add, remove, or reorder patches that layer overrides on the base menu (e.g., pricing adjustments, item availability changes). Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Search for the store by Name , Store Number , or Franchise Code using the search box. Step 3: Once you find the store, click the three dot menu (•••) icon to open the options menu. Step 4: Click Menus from the dropdown menu. This displays the menus assigned to this store. Step 5: Locate the channel you want to edit patches for, and click the more menu button (⋯) on that row. Step 6: Click Edit Patch List from the options menu. Step 7: Add, remove, or reorder patches: Action How to do it Add Patch Click the + Add Patch button and search for the patch by name Remove Patch Click the delete/remove icon next to the patch name Reorder Patches Drag and drop patches to change their order (top = applied first) Step 8: Review the final patch list order. Patches are applied from top to bottom — the topmost patch is applied first. Step 9: Click Save to apply the patch list changes. :::note What are patches? Patches are menu overrides specific to a store. They can adjust item pricing, add/remove items, or change availability for this store only, layered on top of the base menu. ::: :::tip Patch order matters: Patches are applied in order from top to bottom. If patches conflict, the first one in the list takes precedence. ::: :::caution After editing patches, publish the menu to make the changes live to customers. ::: Related guides — See menu assignments — Copy patch list to other stores — Publish changes to customers Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Manages the ordered list of patches applied to a store's menu, allowing operators to add, remove, or reorder patches that layer overrides on the base menu (e.

title: Edit Store Details
body: Edit Store Details What this guide covers Updates an existing store's information such as name, settings, or operational fields. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Search for the store by Name , Store Number , or Franchise Code using the search box. Step 3: Once you find the store, click the three dot menu (•••) icon on the store's row to open the options menu. Step 4: Click Edit from the dropdown menu. Step 5: Update the store fields as needed. Refer to the field descriptions below. All fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Store Name Full display name of the store e.g., “KFC George Street Sydney” Store Number Unique numeric identifier assigned by market operations Must match the POS assigned store number Franchise Code Alphanumeric code identifying the franchisee Provided by your regional manager Time Zone The store's local time zone Required for item snooze and future order accuracy Accepting Online Orders Toggle: Yes or No Set to No during closures or operational issues Appear in Store Search Results Toggle: Yes or No Set to No to hide a location without deleting it Allows Future Orders Toggle: Yes or No Enables customers to place advance orders; requires a supported channel Step 6: Once all changes are complete, the Save button becomes active. Click Save to update the store. :::caution Clicking Cancel at any time discards all unsaved changes. ::: Related guides — Register a new store — Toggle order acceptance separately Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Updates an existing store's information such as name, settings, or operational fields.

title: Edit/Delete a Beneficiary
body: Edit/Delete a Beneficiary What this guide covers Updates or removes an existing beneficiary record, including its name, donation status, and associated stores. Steps Step 1: Navigate to the Stores section using the left hand navigation menu. Step 2: Click the Beneficiaries tab at the top of the Stores page. Step 3: Use the search box to find the beneficiary by name , or scroll through the list. Step 4: Once you find the beneficiary, click the three dot menu (•••) icon on the beneficiary's row to open the options menu. To Edit: Step 5: Click Edit to open the beneficiary details form. Step 6: Update the beneficiary fields as needed. All fields marked with are required. Field What to enter Notes Beneficiary Name Name of the charity or cause e.g., “KFC Youth Foundation” Accepting Donations Toggle: Yes or No Controls whether donations are active Associated Stores Select stores Use search to add or remove stores from this beneficiary Step 7: Once changes are complete, click Save to update the beneficiary. To Delete: Step 5: Click Delete from the options menu. Step 6: Confirm the deletion in the modal that appears by clicking Delete again. This action cannot be undone. :::caution Deleting a beneficiary cannot be reversed. To temporarily disable a beneficiary, set Accepting Donations to No instead. ::: :::caution Clicking Cancel at any time discards unsaved changes (Edit mode only). ::: Related guides — See all beneficiaries linked to a store — Set up a new beneficiary Part of the · Section: Stores
summary: Updates or removes an existing beneficiary record, including its name, donation status, and associated stores.

title: Stores
body: Stores This section covers all stores related tasks in the Byte Commerce Admin Portal, including store setup, menu management, item visibility, and donation handling. Topics Store Setup — Register a new store location — Update store name, location, and settings — Enable or disable digital orders — Show or hide the store from customer search — Enable or disable advance ordering — Manage store group membership Menu Management — See all assigned menus by channel — Link a menu to an ordering channel — Publish menus across multiple stores — Publish from the store’s menu view — Add, remove, or reorder menu patches — Copy patches to other stores — Attach system metadata to menus Item & Availability Management — Temporarily remove items from the menu Store Information & Compliance — Track menu publish jobs and get support details — See active promotions for the store — Review tax rules by store group — Manage point of sale devices and authentication Beneficiary Management — Set up a charity for donation collection — Update or remove beneficiaries — See beneficiaries linked to a store
